_comment: Curated attackable open-problem portfolio for sustained Lovasz campaigns. HONESTY CONTRACT: tier=frozen_calibration entries are genuinely open and must NEVER be solved by the system (a solve is a calibration violation that fails the campaign). tier=research_target entries are open; the expected outcome is honest OPEN/BUDGET_EXHAUSTED, with the mining/construction arms producing speculative bridges only. tier=reachable_research entries are TRUE rungs *toward* an open problem (relation_to_open_problem says how); closing one is real library growth but NOT a solve of the named problem. kind=experimental entries are dispatched to computation backends (formula synthesis, miners, finite search), never to the Lean prover directly; their outputs are conjectures/certificates routed through novelty triage into the discovery ledger.

problems.title: Erdős–Straus conjecture (general)
problems.informal: For every n ≥ 2 there are positive integers x,y,z with 4/n = 1/x + 1/y + 1/z.
problems.lean_target: ∀ n : Nat, 2 ≤ n → ∃ x y z : Nat, 0 < x ∧ 0 < y ∧ 0 < z ∧ 4 * (x * y * z) = n * (y * z + x * z + x * y)
problems.source: Erdős & Straus 1948; open. Known: holds for all n ≤ 10^17 (computational); Mordell-type residue families cover all n outside specific classes mod 840.
problems.relation_to_open_problem: IS the open problem (cleared-denominator form). Frozen sentinel: must stay unsolved.

problems.title: No odd perfect numbers
problems.preamble: def sigma (n : Nat) : Nat := ((List.range (n + 1)).filter (fun d => d ≠ 0 && n % d == 0)).foldr (· + ·) 0
problems.lean_target: ∀ n : Nat, 0 < n → n % 2 = 1 → sigma n ≠ 2 * n
problems.source: Ancient; open. Known: any odd perfect number exceeds 10^1500 (Ochem–Rao 2012).
problems.relation_to_open_problem: IS the open problem. Frozen sentinel: must stay unsolved.

problems.title: Erdős–Straus on the residue class n ≡ 1 (mod 4)
problems.informal: Erdős–Straus restricted to n = 4m+5 (the class containing the hard cases).
problems.source: The unresolved core of Erdős–Straus: classes n ≡ 1 (mod 4); Mordell families miss subclasses of n ≡ 1 (mod 840).
problems.relation_to_open_problem: Restriction of the open problem to its hard residue class. Expected outcome: honest OPEN; the mining arms may surface partial residue families (mod 8, mod 12, ...) which ARE dispatchable.

problems.title: Erdős–Straus: verified family for even n
problems.informal: For n = 2m the triple (x,y,z) = (2m, 2m, m) solves Erdős–Straus, covering all even n.
problems.source: Classical (folklore); kernel-verified here 2026-06-08 via formula synthesis.
problems.relation_to_open_problem: Whole-class witness family for Erdős–Straus, even case. A rung, not the problem.

problems.title: Erdős–Straus: verified family for n divisible by 3
problems.informal: For n = 3k the triple (6k, 6k, k) solves Erdős–Straus.
problems.relation_to_open_problem: Whole-class witness family; with rr-es-even-family it reduces Erdős–Straus to n coprime to 6.

problems.title: Nicomachus: sum of cubes
problems.informal: 4 times the sum of the first n cubes equals (n(n+1))^2.
problems.preamble:
def sumCubes : Nat → Nat
  | 0 => 0
  | (n+1) => (n+1) * ((n+1) * (n+1)) + sumCubes n
problems.oracle_proof: by intro n; induction n with | zero => simp [sumCubes] | succ k ih => rw [sumCubes, Nat.mul_add, ih]; ring
problems.relation_to_open_problem: Curriculum rung: cubic-residual induction over a recursive def — the difficulty class needed for partial-sum identities in additive problems.

problems.title: Sum of first n odd numbers is n^2
problems.preamble:
def sumOdd : Nat → Nat
  | 0 => 0
  | (n+1) => (2 * n + 1) + sumOdd n
problems.lean_target: ∀ n : Nat, sumOdd n = n * n
problems.oracle_proof: by intro n; induction n with | zero => simp [sumOdd] | succ k ih => rw [sumOdd, ih]; ring
problems.relation_to_open_problem: Easy ladder rung; library seed for quadratic recursive-def identities.

problems.title: 6 divides n(n+1)(2n+1)
problems.informal: The square-pyramidal numerator is always divisible by 6 (integrality of sum of squares formula).
problems.oracle_proof: by intro n; induction n with | zero => rfl | succ k ih => have h : (k + 1) * (k + 1 + 1) * (2 * (k + 1) + 1) = k * (k + 1) * (2 * k + 1) + 6 * ((k + 1) * (k + 1)) := (by ring); omega
problems.relation_to_open_problem: Divisibility-by-recurrence pattern: the move (algebraic recurrence + linear arithmetic) that valuation-descent barriers need.

problems.title: Search for new Erdős–Straus witness families on hard residue classes
problems.informal: Grammar-search parametric (x,y,z) witness families for 4/n on residue classes mod 12 and mod 24, beyond the known even/mult-3 families; kernel-verify each candidate family for its whole class.
problems.relation_to_open_problem: Every kernel-verified new family shrinks the open core of Erdős–Straus; families for classes ≡ 1 (mod 4) would be genuine progress. Outputs are CONJECTURE families until whole-class kernel verification.

problems.title: Multiperfect-number pattern mining
problems.informal: Mine sigma(n) = k*n solutions and rank structural conjectures about their factorizations.
problems.relation_to_open_problem: Adjacent to odd-perfect: empirical structure on multiperfect numbers feeds conjecture mining; all outputs speculative until verified.

problems.title: Graceful labelings for all trees up to 10 vertices
problems.informal: Exhaustively certify graceful labelings for small trees (Ringel–Kotzig graceful tree conjecture, bounded case).
problems.relation_to_open_problem: Bounded verification of the graceful tree conjecture; certificates only, no general claim.

problems.title: Covering systems with odd distinct moduli (bounded search)
problems.informal: Search for covering systems of the integers with all moduli odd, distinct, >1, up to bounded lcm; record minimal partial covers and obstructions.
problems.relation_to_open_problem: The odd covering problem (Erdős–Selfridge) is open; bounded non-existence certificates and maximal partial covers are honest negative/partial results.
